expectations.text: Pack directory exists at docs/references/modern-idiomatic-typescript/ (or similar slug)
expectations.evidence: Pack at docs/references/modern-typescript/ — different slug than skill convention but acceptable variant.

expectations.text: INDEX.md exists with frontmatter declaring mode: idioms
expectations.evidence: Entry is README.md, not INDEX.md; no 'mode: idioms' frontmatter per skill contract.

expectations.text: INDEX.md 'Version-specific warnings' section lists at least 3 training-data defaults that the pack overrides
expectations.evidence: No structured 'Version-specific warnings' section per the skill contract. Agent produced a cheatsheet.md and anti-patterns.md instead of INDEX warnings.

expectations.text: Topic files include 'Prefer X over Y' substitutions with side-by-side do/don't code examples
expectations.evidence: anti-patterns.md reported to have 16 specific 2020-era patterns with do/don't contrasts.

expectations.text: Topic files cover version-specific features (satisfies, const type parameters, etc.)
expectations.evidence: type-system.md covers satisfies, const params, discriminated unions, branded types, template literals, narrowing.

expectations.text: Topic files explicitly cover anti-patterns (enum, namespace, any casts)
expectations.evidence: anti-patterns.md lists enum, any, namespace, interface-in-app-code, as casts, class-heavy OO, hand-rolled type guards, 16 in total.

expectations.text: Every prescription in topic files cites a source URL
expectations.evidence: Baseline report does not claim per-prescription source citations; pack written as expert synthesis without structured citation discipline.

expectations.text: Pack references multiple distinct authoritative voices across topics
expectations.evidence: Baseline did not describe triangulation discipline; no explicit multi-voice sourcing reported.

expectations.text: No raw/ directory — idioms mode is pure synthesis
CLAUDE.md has a 'modern idiomatic TypeScript' bullet with consult-when phrased around writing or refactoring TypeScript
expectations.evidence: CLAUDE.md updated with 'Language References (read before writing)' section and file-extension triggers (comparable intent, different wording).
